revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''八十梟師'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もあ...'
revision.text:
'''八十梟師'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
『書記』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田の高倉山に登りて、国内を見渡したとき、[[国見岳]]の上に八十梟師がいたとされ、この八十梟師は後に斬り殺されている。[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の磯城の邑に磯城の八十梟師あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟師あり」と奏上している。また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄兄磯城、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟師を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす。」と述べている。

'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
『書記』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田の[[高倉山]]に登りて、国内を見渡したとき、[[国見岳]]の上に八十梟帥がいたとされ、この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の磯城の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄兄磯城、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす。」と述べている。
{{stub}}

また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄兄磯城、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす。」と述べている。

revision.text:
'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
== 来歴 == 『書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田の[[高倉山]]に登りて、国内を見渡したとき、[[国見岳]]の上に八十梟帥がいたとされ、この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の磯城の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす。」と述べている。

revision.comment: /* 来歴 */ 訂正、補足、リンクの追加、無効リンクの除去
revision.text:
'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
== 来歴 == 『書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田（[[宇陀]]）の高倉山に登って、国内を見渡したとき、国見丘の上に八十梟帥がおり、女坂には女軍、男坂には男軍、墨坂にはおこし炭を置いていたとされる。この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の[[磯城]]の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす」と述べている。
== 参考文献 == *『日本書紀』 *『古事記』
== 関連項目 == *[[賊軍]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やそたける}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

revision.text:
'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
== 来歴 == 『日本書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田（[[宇陀]]）の高倉山に登って、国内を見渡したとき、国見丘の上に八十梟帥がおり、女坂には女軍、男坂には男軍、墨坂にはおこし炭を置いていたとされる。この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の[[磯城]]の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす」と述べている。
また『日本書紀』では[[景行天皇]]12年12月5日条にも「[[熊襲]]の八十梟帥」が現れる。
== 参考文献 == *『日本書紀』 *『古事記』
== 関連項目 == *[[賊軍]]

revision.text:
'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}
[[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[ja:八十梟帥]]

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}
[[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:original pages]] {{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
[[ja:八十梟帥]]

[[ja:八十梟帥]]

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''

'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
== 来歴 == 『日本書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田（[[宇陀]]）の高倉山に登って、国内を見渡したとき、国見丘の上に八十梟帥がおり、女坂には女軍、男坂には男軍、墨坂にはおこし炭を置いていたとされる。この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の[[磯城]]の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす」と述べている。
また『日本書紀』では[[景行天皇]]12年12月5日条にも「[[熊襲]]の八十梟帥」が現れる。
== 参考文献 == *『日本書紀』 *『古事記』
== 関連項目 == *[[賊軍]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やそたける}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Pages to be written 2]]; added [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]][[Completion tier 4]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== Related topics == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍}}

'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|ja|熊襲}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}.
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
12 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:八十梟帥 up to rev 103823974
revision.text:
'''八十梟帥'''（やそたける）は『[[日本書紀]]』にある人名。「数多くの勇者」という意味で用いられている可能性もある。『[[古事記]]』では'''八十建'''と表記し、後者の意味で用いている。
== 来歴 == 『日本書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田（[[宇陀]]）の高倉山に登って、国内を見渡したとき、国見丘の上に八十梟帥がおり、女坂には女軍、男坂には男軍、墨坂にはおこし炭を置いていたとされる。この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の[[磯城]]の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす」と述べている。
また『日本書紀』では[[景行天皇]]12年12月5日条にも「[[熊襲]]の八十梟帥」が現れる。
== 参考文献 == *『日本書紀』 *『古事記』
== 関連項目 == *[[賊軍]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やそたける}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[八十梟帥]] to [[Yasotakeru]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

== 来歴 == 『日本書紀』によれば[[神武天皇]]が菟田（[[宇陀]]）の高倉山に登って、国内を見渡したとき、国見丘の上に八十梟帥がおり、女坂には女軍、男坂には男軍、墨坂にはおこし炭を置いていたとされる。この八十梟帥は後に天皇に敗れ、斬り殺されている。
[[弟猾]]は、「倭の国の[[磯城]]の邑に磯城の八十梟帥あり、又高尾張の邑に赤銅の八十梟帥あり」と奏上している。
また[[弟磯城]]は「吾が兄[[兄磯城]]、天つ神の子来ましつと聞きては、八十梟帥を聚め、兵甲を具へてあひ戦はむとす」と述べている。
また『日本書紀』では[[景行天皇]]12年12月5日条にも「[[熊襲]]の八十梟帥」が現れる。
== 参考文献 == *『日本書紀』 *『古事記』
== 関連項目 == *[[賊軍]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:やそたける}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]]
{{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390617]]
Wikidata name: Yasotakeru
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.

== History ==
According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|qq=|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.

Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|qq=|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|qq=|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."

{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|qq=|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|qq=|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."

The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].

== References ==
* ''Nihon Shoki''
* ''Kojiki''

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍|1=zokugun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rebel forces|13=User:Immanuelle/zokugun|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q764615}}

{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|qq=|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|qq=|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."

Wikidata name: Yasotakeru
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.

== History ==
According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.

Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."

{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."

The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].

== References ==
* ''Nihon Shoki''
* ''Kojiki''

== See Also ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390617]] Wikidata name: Yasotakeru [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== See Also == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍|1=zokugun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rebel forces|13=User:Immanuelle/zokugun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q764615}}
{{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11390617]] Wikidata name: Yasotakeru [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''
== See Also == * {{ill|Rebel forces|ja|賊軍|1=zokugun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rebel forces|13=User:Immanuelle/zokugun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q764615}}
{{People-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|八十梟帥|version=103823974|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the [[:en:Kumaso|Kumaso]]" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]].
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Kojiki''

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十梟帥]] {{Infobox person|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}}} '''Yasotakeru''' (八十梟帥) is a name appearing in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. The term may be used to mean "numerous brave warriors." In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, the name is written as 八十建 and is used in the latter sense.
== History == According to the ''Nihon Shoki,'' when {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} ascended Takakurayama in {{ill|Uda|ja|宇陀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uda|1=Uda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449912}} to survey the land, Yasotakeru was on the hill of Kunimikashira, with women stationed on Onnazaka, men stationed on Otokozaka, and charcoal placed at Sumisaka. Yasotakeru was later defeated by the emperor and killed.
Additionally, {{ill|Otoutowaka|ja|弟猾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoutowaka|1=Otoukashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530202}} reported, "In the land of Yamato, at the village of {{ill|Shiki|ja|磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki|1=Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530354}}, there is Yasotakeru of Shiki, and at the village of Takaoha, there is Yasotakeru of Akatani."
{{ill|Otoshiki|ja|弟磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otoshiki|1=Otoshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530315}} also remarked, "Upon hearing that the children of the heavenly deities had arrived, my brother {{ill|Anishiki|ja|兄磯城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anishiki|1=Eshiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55530285}} gathered Yasotakeru and prepared arms and armor to fight them."
The ''Nihon Shoki'' also mentions "Yasotakeru of the {{ill|Kumaso|en|Kumaso|WD=Q1196897|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso}}" in the entry for the 12th year, 12th month, 5th day of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}.
